name.es: Hot Cross Buns (panecillos de la cruz)
name.fr: Hot Cross Buns (petits pains de Pâques)

description.en: Spiced English yeast buns studded with currants and candied peel, marked with a piped cross and glazed sticky-sweet. Baked every Good Friday to mark the end of Lent, they're split, toasted and buttered all through the Easter season.
description.de: Englische Hefebrötchen mit Gewürzen, Korinthen und kandierter Schale, mit einem aufgespritzten Kreuz markiert und klebrig-süß glasiert. Traditionell am Karfreitag zum Ende der Fastenzeit gebacken und die ganze Osterzeit über getoastet genossen.
description.es: Panecillos ingleses de levadura con especias, pasas de Corinto y cáscara confitada, marcados con una cruz de pasta y glaseados. Se hornean cada Viernes Santo al cerrar la Cuaresma, y se comen tostados con mantequilla durante toda la Pascua.
description.fr: Petits pains anglais briochés aux épices, aux raisins de Corinthe et aux écorces confites, marqués d'une croix pochée et glacés. Cuits chaque Vendredi saint pour clore le Carême, dégustés toastés et beurrés pendant toute la période pascale.

origin.note: The cross recalls the crucifixion; the mixed spice was traditionally likened to the spices used to prepare Christ's body for burial. Eaten to mark the end of the Good Friday/Lenten fast.

ingredients.note: strong white bread flour, for the dough
or currants, the more traditional British choice
mixed candied orange and lemon peel
for the piped flour-water cross; not counted toward the dough's flour percentage

keeping.note: Good for a few days and excellent toasted after that; the dried fruit keeps them moister than a plain roll.

method.title: Soak the fruit
method.summary: Steep the currants or raisins and candied peel in hot tea while you prepare the dough, then drain well.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine flour, mixed spice, cinnamon, salt, sugar and lemon zest, then work in the milk, yeast, butter, egg and yolk into a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: Add the fruit
method.summary: Knead the drained fruit and peel through the dough until it's evenly distributed.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough double in a warm spot, about 1.5-2 hours.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Divide into 12 even balls, set them close together in a baking pan, and let them proof again until puffy, up to an hour.

method.title: Pipe the cross
method.summary: Pipe a thick flour-water paste in strips across each bun to mark the traditional cross.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake at around 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes until golden.

method.title: Glaze
method.summary: Brush the warm buns with a simmered sugar-water syrup for their characteristic sticky shine.

source.adaptation: formula.* covers the dough only. The piped flour-water cross and post-bake sugar glaze are itemized but excluded from the flour total and from hydration/sugar/fat percentages.
